items.final_wording: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

source.title: Pneumonia: diagnosis and management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
source.setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

items.rationale: Supports pneumonia recovery advice and when to seek further care for respiratory safety-net documentation; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed asthma warning prompts for respiratory safety-net documentation; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46
source.population: People presenting with cough; age limits are not stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth cough documentation; only partial for a general in-person workflow and for chronic-cough investigation pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed cough warning prompts for respiratory safety-net documentation; no decision is generated.
